relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks holiness into everyday life.
Be holy, because YHWH your Divine is holy.
Revere mother and father; keep Sabbath.
Do not turn to made gods.
Peace offerings are brought for acceptance.
The holy meal has its time.
Keeping the boundary preserves acceptance.
Profaning the holy meal carries consequence.
Harvest is not stripped to the edge.
Gleanings remain for poor and sojourner.
Do not steal, deceive, or lie.
Do not use the Name falsely.
Do not oppress or withhold wages overnight.
Do not curse the deaf or trip the blind; fear Divine.
Justice must not bend toward poverty or greatness.
Do not slander or stand over your companion's blood/life.
Do not nurse hatred; address harm truthfully.
Do not avenge or keep grudge; love your neighbor as yourself.
Creation-boundary signs are kept in herd, field, and garment.
An unfree woman's case exposes power, harm, and need for repair.
Reparation is brought before YHWH.
Covering is made for the sin/misalignment.
New fruit trees wait through three years.
The fourth year's fruit becomes praise to YHWH.
The fifth year's fruit is eaten with increase.
Blood/life and omens are not consumed as control.
Hair and beard boundaries mark covenant identity.
Body marks for the dead are guarded in this ritual identity.
A daughter must not be profaned through exploitation.
Sabbath and sanctuary are revered.
Do not seek spirit-powers that make unclean.
Rise before gray hair and honor elders.
Do not wrong the sojourner in your land.
Love the sojourner as yourself, because you were sojourners in Egypt.
Do not distort measure, weight, or quantity.
Honest measures remember the One who brought you out of Egypt.
Keep and do the statutes and judgments of YHWH.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 19 is holiness with dirt under its fingernails: leave food for the poor, pay workers, protect disabled people, tell truth, love neighbor, love sojourner, honor elders, and measure honestly.
